title: scrophularia canina subsp. frutescens
intro: Scrophularia canina subsp. frutescens, also known as the woodland figwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the figwort family, Scrophulariaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe and calm the body. The medicinal properties of Scrophularia canina subsp. frutescens have been associated with various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as infusions and tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
